w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: ased Calculator for Postfix Notation with Binary Search Tree

The goal of this assignment is to implement a text-based calculator that can evaluate arithmetic expressions in postfix notation using a stack data structure and handle variables using a binary search tree (BST). You will design and implement an array-based stack,
algorithm to evaluate postfix expressions, handle integer operands and basic arithmetic operations, integrate a binary search tree to store variables and their values,
display the binary search tree, and handle deletion of all variables. The correctness of your implementation can be verified by following the provided
test cases in the sample runs.
Complete the case exercises provided in the
This is a team assignment. Work in groups of three
hours and you’re expected to complete it outside of scheduled hours.
below to ensure your program meets the specifications.
Ensure your program meets the requirements in the
In addition to writing the code, you are required to present your code to the instructor. Refer to the
section for details on how you will be assessed
Individually, complete a Peer Assessment for your team. Review the
ubmit your completed assignment according to the
date and time stated by your instructor to
Implement the provided ADT stack interface using an array-based implementation. The stack interface should support operations such as
Implement a BST to store variables and their values. The binary search tree should support operations such as
that takes a string representing a postfix arithmetic expression and returns
The calculator should handle integer operands and
support the following operations: addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*) and division (/).
Each operand and operator in the expression
be separated by spaces. For example,
If an operand is encountered, push it onto the stack. If a variable is encountered, search for its value in the binary search tree and push the value onto the stack.
If an operator is encountered, pop the top two operands from the stack. Perform the operation using the operator and operands. Push the result back onto the stack.
If an operand or variable is encountered, store it in the binary search tree with its value using the insert operation.
steps until the expression is fully evaluated.
will be the top element on the stack.
Must organize your code solution into appropriate packages to promote a modular and structured approach.
Do not use any built-in Java utilities or libraries that directly evaluate postfix expressions.
Implement the stack data structure, binary search tree, and postfix evaluation algorithm from scratch.
Adhere to good coding practices, including
any possible errors or exceptions that may occur during the execution of the program.
Before starting the implementation, make sure you understand the concept of postfix notation, how the evaluation algorithm works and how binary search trees operate.
solving a few postfix expressions manually to familiarize yourself with the process.
dynamic array to implement the stack data structure
etc., to manage the stack operations.
data structures and variables to store operands,
and intermediate results during the evaluation process.
) to manage variables and their values.
If a token is an operand, check if it is a variable by searching for its key in the
. If the variable is found, push its corresponding value onto the stack. Otherwise,
it as an integer operand and
In addition to developing your code,

you will have the opportunity to showcase your understanding of the assignment, the implementation details, and your problem-solving approach. Your presentation should be clear, concise, and structured to effectively communicate your
: articulate the problem your code is addressing.
escribe any challenges or constraints your team encountered.
: present the high-level architecture of your project. Discuss the major components and modules within your code.
alk through the structure and organization of your code. Highlight key classes, functions, and data structures
Each team member must complete a peer assessment of their group members and submit
with the same title as the “Your

”. The purpose of this peer review is to offer constructive feedback to your peers. Please utilize the template provided below to assign ratings to each of your group members on a scale of 1 to 3, where:
2 = Acceptable but with some areas for improvement
a key field to store the variable name
and a value field to store the variable
should have fields for the key
references to the left and right child nodes.
Implement an insertion method that takes the variable name (key) and its value as input and inserts a new node with the given key-value pair into the BST
. Ensure that the tree maintains the
property, i.e., all keys in the left subtree are less than the current node's key, and all keys in the right subtree are greater than the current node's key.
Implement a search method that takes a key as input and returns the value associated with that key if found in the BST
. Perform a binary search starting from the root node and traversing left or right based on the key comparison.
Implement a deletion method that takes a key as input and removes the node with the given key from the BST
. Handle different cases, such as deleting a node with no children, deleting a node with one
and deleting a node with two children. Ensure that the tree remains a valid binary search tree after deletion.
that deletes all nodes from the
that displays the binary search tree in a hierarchical structure. This will help visualize the arrangement of nodes in the tree. You
and a loop to achieve this.
to store variables and their values.
An instance variable named stack of type
to implement the stack for the postfix evaluation.

that takes a string representing a postfix arithmetic expression and returns the result of the evaluation as an integer. This method should implement the steps described earlier to evaluate the expression using a stack and handle variables using the binary search tree.
that sets a variable with its corresponding value in the binary search tree.
that deletes all variables from the binary search tree.
any additional helper methods required for the proper functioning of the postfix calculator.
ed in the following sample runs
After evaluating each expression, delete all variables using the
display the binary search tree using the
When your program begins, it should display a
the purpose of the program and
prompt asking you to press OK to initiate the program!

w:r.w:t: s the step-by-step explanation of the BST visualization:
The postfix expression being evaluated is g h
The BST is constructed based on the variables used in the expression and their respective values.
The root node of the BST is the node labeled j with a value of 5. It is represented as
The left child of the root node is the node labeled
with a value of 4. It is represented as
is the node labeled h with a value of 3. It is represented as
The left child of the h node is the node labeled g with a value of 2. It is represented as
Since there are no further left children, the right subtree of the g node is displayed as empty.
The remaining part of the tree is displayed in a similar manner.
node is the rightmost node labeled j with a value of 5. It is represented as
her right children, the left subtree of the j node is displayed as empty.
The resulting value of the postfix expression g h
+ * j / is 2, which is displayed as Result: 2.
To ensure an organized submission process, please follow these instructions carefully:
Begin by compiling your Java program and creating a JAR file. This JAR file should contain all the necessary compiled classes and resources essential for running your program effectively.
Assemble all the required files into a single ZIP archive. This archive should consist of the following items:
Place the generated JAR file, containing your Java program, inside the ZIP archive. Ensure that the JAR file includes all the dependencies and resources required for the program to execute correctly.
Include a small readme file within the ZIP archive. This file should provide clear instructions on how to run your program successfully.
Arrange the files inside the ZIP archive in a well-structured manner. Consider creating separate directories or folders for organization and clarity.
an extremely serious academic offense. Reference Procedure AC. 3.4.1 – Student Code of Conduct (available on
Be prepared to share an explanation of your solutions. Your instructor may select any team to provide an explanation to any question.
Incorrect or incomplete implementation of stack operations.
Correct implementation of essential stack operations (push, pop, peek,
Adequate handling of stack size and resizing.
Precise implementation of stack operations with optimal efficiency
Effective management of stack size and resizing.
Incorrect or incomplete implementation of BST operations.
Accurate implementation of BST operations (insert, search, delete,
Proper maintenance of binary search tree property.
Flawless implementation of BST operations with exceptional precision.
Robust maintenance of binary search tree property.
Inaccurate handling of operands, operators, and variables.
Proper handling of operands, operators, and variables in expressions.
Accurate evaluation of postfix expressions using stack and BST.
Adequate variable lookup and operand handling.
Flawless handling of operands, operators, and variables.
Precise evaluation of postfix expressions using stack and BST.
Optimal variable lookup and operand handling.
Disregard for coding practices, inconsistent indentation.
Adherence to coding practices: proper indentation, meaningful variable names, and comments
Well-structured code with clear use of helper methods.
Exemplary coding practices: consistent indentation, descriptive variable names, and detailed comments.
Highly organized code with optimized use of helper methods.
Thorough testing of various postfix expressions, including those with variables.
Correct display of binary search tree in sample runs.
Accurate evaluation results and variable deletion handling.
Comprehensive testing, including extensive coverage of edge cases.
Precise display of binary search tree in sample runs.
Perfectly accurate evaluation results and variable deletion handling.
Limited or unclear explanation of the problem, challenges, and project architecture. (6 marks)
Provides a clear explanation of the problem, challenges, and project architecture. (12 marks)
Presents a compelling and engaging overview, covering all aspects effectively. (
